regimeNotes: NEUTRAL regime maintained 2026-07-20; caution flags persist but with a slightly more mixed picture than July 15. Geopolitics/oil: the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz (reinstated July 14) remains in force with ongoing CENTCOM strikes on Iranian assets; WTI holding near $80, still elevated vs. the pre-crisis ~$69 but not re-escalating further this week -- a persistent but stable risk rather than a fresh shock. Rates: 10Y yield eased to ~4.50-4.57% (from 4.62% peak) on softer inflation prints and a cooling labor market -- a mild risk-on signal. DXY ~100.7, flat/soft. Housing showing cracks (pending home sales -5.4% in June, 30Y mortgage 6.55%), a growth-drag watch item. Equities: pulled back modestly from last week's near-record highs -- SPY $746.27 (from $751.83), NVDA $205.24 (from $211.80), META fell -2.79% Friday to $646 on top of other softness; VIX rose to ~18.8 (+12% w/w, from ~16.5), still under the 20 stress line but the move itself is a caution signal. AI-adjacent EDA names (CDNS, SNPS) sold off hard on disruption fears (AI-designed chips). Crypto: total mcap ~$2.28T (-0.23% 24h, down slightly from $2.30T), BTC dominance ~56.4% (flat), BTC $64,355 (roughly flat vs. $64,777), ETH $1,873.71, SOL $76.41. Fear & Greed improved to 29 'Fear' (from 25 'Extreme Fear') -- sentiment recovering slightly even as prices dipped, a mild positive divergence. NAV $99,965.18 (-0.93% vs July 15's $100,905.64, first down week in a month, still +/-0% vs $100k inception-adjusted). Crypto sleeve drifted to ~20.03% of NAV (BTC/ETH firm vs. a slightly lower stock book), at the 20% neutral cap -- trimmed 3 SOL (~$229) to bring it to ~19.8% with a buffer. Cash 13.94% of NAV, comfortably above the 5% floor. No position exceeds the 15% entry cap or the ~20% drift-trim trigger (MSFT largest at 14.89%). No invalidation levels breached. Net read: easing yields and improving crypto sentiment are mild risk-on offsets against a rising VIX, a still-unresolved Hormuz blockade, and cooling housing data -- net-net insufficient conviction either direction. REGIME UNCHANGED (neutral). Only action today: guardrail-driven SOL trim to hold the crypto sleeve under cap; otherwise a HOLD day.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta at $746.27 (above $741.75 avgCost; +0.61%, ~13.08% NAV). Index pulled back modestly off record highs as VIX rose to ~18.8 amid the persistent Hormuz blockade, but still well above avgCost and yields easing is a mild offset. Broad index remains the diversified anchor. HOLD, watch for technical deterioration if VIX breaks 20 or the geopolitical situation worsens.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed multi-hike path forcing a technical downtrend in the broad index.

NVDA.thesis: AI/compute secular leader at $205.24 (below $208.44 avgCost; -1.54%, ~14.28% NAV). Pulled back with the broader AI/semis complex this week (AI-EDA disruption fears hit CDNS/SNPS) but the capex/data-center demand narrative is intact; still near the 15% cap -- no further adds. HOLD.
NVDA.invalidationLevel: AI capex digestion or data-center demand rollover; close below 150 USD.

MSFT.thesis: Quality AI/cloud compounder at $391.00 (below 391.98 avgCost; -0.25%, ~14.89% NAV). At the 15% position cap -- no further adds. Azure + Copilot growth thesis unchanged. HOLD.
MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.thesis: Quality growth (search + cloud + AI) at $347.74 (below 366.53 avgCost; -5.13%, ~13.67% NAV). Search monopoly thesis intact; AI integration ongoing; pullback with the broader mega-cap tech complex, not thesis-specific. HOLD.
GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals causing revenue decline; close below 310 USD.

UNH.thesis: Defensive value at $426.09 (above $408.52 avgCost; +4.30%, ~6.26% NAV). Holding gains, still acting as a natural hedge against ongoing geopolitical/rate volatility. HOLD.
UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

GLD.thesis: Tail/debasement hedge at $369.16 (below 386.54 avgCost; -4.50%, ~4.08% NAV). Easing 10Y yields (~4.50-4.57%, down from the 4.62% peak) are a modest tailwind, but gold still lagging avgCost. Not adding here. HOLD.
GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with strong USD reversal sustained; close below 350 USD.

BTC.thesis: Core crypto sleeve leader at $64,355 (below 64,629 avgCost; -0.42%, ~14.44% NAV). Roughly flat vs. avgCost; Fear & Greed improved to 29 'Fear' (from 25 'Extreme Fear') even as price dipped slightly -- a mild positive sentiment divergence. BTC dominance ~56.4%, stable. Crypto sleeve trimmed back to ~19.8% (neutral cap, buffer); no room to add. HOLD.

ETH.thesis: Discounted L1 leader at $1,873.71 (above 1,667.47 avgCost; +12.37%, ~4.50% NAV). Continuing to hold gains and outperform its avgCost. Asymmetric upside intact when risk-on returns and crypto cap expands to 35%. HOLD.
ETH.invalidationLevel: Structural loss of share to L1 rivals; close below 1,300 USD.

SOL.thesis: High-beta satellite at $76.41 (above 67.81 avgCost; +12.68%, ~0.86% NAV after today's trim). Trimmed 3 SOL (~$229) to bring the crypto sleeve from ~20.03% back to ~19.8%, under the 20% neutral cap with a buffer; smallest-conviction sleeve name, first to trim on cap breaches. HOLD remainder for further upside on regime flip.
SOL.invalidationLevel: Break below 52 USD; network deterioration or sentiment collapse.
